content:
I Thought Loyalty Would Pay Off. I Was Wrong.

17 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Free Link
Recently, one of my subscribers shared a story with me that I believe many professionals, especially those working in the software industry, will relate to. Although this story is about one individual, I suspect thousands of people silently experience something similar every year. The names have been changed, but the lesson remains exactly the same.
Let’s call him Praveen.
Praveen had been working at the same company for over nine years. Like many fresh graduates, he joined the organization with excitement and the hope of building a long-term career. During those nine years, he wasn’t the kind of employee who kept switching teams every few months or spent his time looking for opportunities outside. Instead, he focused on becoming someone the company could rely on.
When he joined, his annual salary was ₹14 lakh. Through yearly appraisals, promotions, and consistent performance, his salary gradually increased to ₹23 lakh over the next nine years. If you look at these numbers in isolation, nothing appears unusual. His salary was growing, he had job security, and he was respected within his team.
